name: tangled-game-engine

metadata.name: tangled-game-engine
metadata.summary: A package for the Tangled game engine and support classes.

documentation:
# tangled-game-package

## Version: 2.2.0

modules.name: tangled_game_engine.tangled_game.game_types
documentation: Support types for the Tangled Game Engine.

classes.name: tangled_game_engine.tangled_game.game_types.InvalidMoveError
documentation: An exception for an invalid move in the game.

classes.name: tangled_game_engine.tangled_game.game_types.InvalidPlayerError
documentation: An exception for an invalid player trying to join the game.

classes.name: tangled_game_engine.tangled_game.game_types.InvalidGameStateError
documentation: An exception for mismatch between a game state and a Game.

classes.name: tangled_game_engine.tangled_game.game_types.Vertex

methods.name: tangled_game_engine.tangled_game.game_types.Vertex.__init__
documentation: Set a vertex in the game graph.

inner_classes.name: tangled_game_engine.tangled_game.game_types.Vertex.State
documentation:
The state of a vertex.
Use this to set or interpret the state of a vertex

Values:
    NONE (int): The vertex has no state (no ownership).
    P1 (int): The vertex is owned by player 1.
    P2 (int): The vertex is owned by player 2.

A vertex in the game graph.

Members:
    state (Vertex.State): The state of the vertex.
    id (int): The id of the vertex.

Enums:
    State: The state of a vertex.

classes.name: tangled_game_engine.tangled_game.game_types.Edge

methods.name: tangled_game_engine.tangled_game.game_types.Edge.__init__
documentation: Set an edge in the game graph.

inner_classes.name: tangled_game_engine.tangled_game.game_types.Edge.State
documentation:
The state of an edge.
Use this to set or interpret the state of an edge.

Values:
    NONE (int): The edge has not been set.
    NEITHER (int): The edge has no state preference.
    FM (int): The edge prefers FM.
    AFM (int): The edge prefers AFM.

An edge in the game graph.

Members:
    vertices (Tuple[int, int]): The two vertices that the edge connects.
    state (Edge.State): The state of the edge.

Enums:
    State: The state of an edge.

modules.name: tangled_game_engine.tangled_game.game
documentation:
Game class for the Tangled game.
The game is played on a graph with vertices and edges. Players take turns 
setting edge states and claiming a node. The game is over when all edges are 
claimed and both players have a node.

The player who has selected the node with the highest influence score wins 
the game.

classes.name: tangled_game_engine.tangled_game.game.Game

methods.name: tangled_game_engine.tangled_game.game.Game.__init__
documentation: Initialize the game.

methods.name: tangled_game_engine.tangled_game.game.Game.get_player_node
documentation:
Helper function. Return the node that the player, identified by index,
owns, or -1 if none.

Args:
    player_index (int): The player index to check (1 or 2). Default is
        0 for either player.

methods.name: tangled_game_engine.tangled_game.game.Game.is_my_turn
documentation:
Return True if it is the player's turn, False otherwise.

Args:
    player_id (str): The player ID to check.

methods.name: tangled_game_engine.tangled_game.game.Game.is_game_over
documentation:
Return True if the game is over. This is the case iff all edges are claimed and both players have a node.

Returns:
    bool: True if the game is over, False otherwise.

methods.name: tangled_game_engine.tangled_game.game.Game.create_game
documentation:
Initialize the game with the token and number of vertices and edges.

Args:
    num_vertices (int): The number of vertices in the game
    edges (List[Tuple[int, int]]): The edges in the game as vertex
        pairs. Edge vertices must be unique, in ascending order, and 
        within the vertex range.

methods.name: tangled_game_engine.tangled_game.game.Game.join_game
documentation:
Join the game with the player_id and player_num.

Args:
    player_id (str): The player ID
    player_num (int): The player number, either 1 or 2, or 0 for either player

methods.name: tangled_game_engine.tangled_game.game.Game.get_game_state
documentation:
Return the game state as a dictionary.

Returns:
    {
        "num_nodes": int,
         # List of edges as vertex pairs and edge state
        "edges": List[Tuple[int, int, int]],
        "graph_id": str,
        "player1_id": str,
        "player2_id": str,
        "turn_count": int,
        "current_player_index": int (1 or 2),
        "player1_node": int, # -1 if no node
        "player2_node": int, # -1 if no node
    }

methods.name: tangled_game_engine.tangled_game.game.Game.set_game_state
documentation:
Set the game state from a dictionary. Validate that it matches the 
current game structure, if requested.

Args:
    state (Dict[str, any]): The game state to set.
        Format:
        {
            "num_nodes": int,
            "edges": List[Tuple[int, int, int]],
            "player1_id": str,
            "player2_id": str,
            "turn_count": int,
            "current_player_index": int (1 or 2),
            "player1_node": int, # -1 if no node
            "player2_node": int, # -1 if no node
        }
    validate (bool): Validate the game state by confirming matching 
        playfield dimensions.
        If False, set the game state without validation, overwriting 
        dimensions to match the state.
        If True, validate that the passed state matches the current 
        game dimensions, and raise an error if not.
        Default is True.

methods.name: tangled_game_engine.tangled_game.game.Game.get_legal_moves_by_index
documentation:
Return a list of legal moves for the player index.

Args:
    player_index (str): The player index to get legal moves for (1 or 2).

Returns:
    List[List[int, int, int]]: A list of legal moves for the player.
        Each move is a list of three integers: move type, move index, and move state.
        Move types are from the MoveType enum.
        Move indices are the vertex or edge index.
        Move states are from the Vertex.State or Edge.State enums.

methods.name: tangled_game_engine.tangled_game.game.Game.get_legal_moves
documentation:
Return a list of legal moves for the player.

Args:
    player_id (str): The player ID to get legal moves for.

Returns:
    List[List[int, int, int]]: A list of legal moves for the player.
        Each move is a list of three integers: move type, move index, and move state.
        Move types are from the MoveType enum.
        Move indices are the vertex or edge index.
        Move states are from the Vertex.State or Edge.State enums.

methods.name: tangled_game_engine.tangled_game.game.Game.make_move
documentation:
Make a move in the game.

Args:
    player_id (str): The player ID making the move.
    move_type (int): The type of move to make (see Game.MoveType).
    move_index (int): The index of the move (vertex or edge).
    move_state (int): The state of the move (see Vertex.State or Edge.State).

Example:
    game.make_move("player1", Game.MoveType.NODE.value, 0, Vertex.State.P1.value)

class_variables.name: tangled_game_engine.tangled_game.game.Game.current_player_index
documentation: Player index who's turn it is (1 or 2)

inner_classes.name: tangled_game_engine.tangled_game.game.Game.MoveType

class_variables.name: tangled_game_engine.tangled_game.game.Game.MoveType.NONE
documentation: No legal moves or it's not the player's turn

The type of move that can be made in the game.

Values:
    NONE (int): No legal moves or it's not the player's turn.
    NODE (int): Claim a node (only once per player per game).
    EDGE (int): Set a preferred edge state.
    QUIT (int): Quit the game.

The game class for the Tangled game. The game is played on a graph with 
vertices and edges. Players take turns setting edge states and claiming a 
node. The game is over when all edges are claimed and both players have
a node. The player who has selected the node with the highest influence 
score wins the game.

Members:
    vertices (List[Vertex]): The vertices in the game graph.
    edges (List[Edge]): The edges in the game graph.
    player1_id (str): The ID of player 1.
    player2_id (str): The ID of player 2.
    turn_count (int): The number of turns taken in the game.
    current_player_index (int): The index of the current player (1 or 2).

Enums:
    MoveType: The type of move that can be made in the game
